title: mosla japonica var. thymolifera
intro: Mosla japonica var. thymolifera, also known as Japanese mountain mint, is a perennial herb native to East Asia, characterized by its fragrant leaves and stems that resemble peppermint. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mosla japonica var. thymolifera include relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and treating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote digestion and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
